campbell.title: Campbell City Council — February 3, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to approve a $4,000 donation from Water Tower Kitchen to help fund youth recreation scholarships. They also discussed extending a temporary local rule and moving forward with plans for a bicycle bridge over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue, including submitting grant applications to help pay for it.
keyTopics: Water Tower Kitchen donates $4,000 to youth recreation scholarships
Extending interim ordinance 2326 (temporary local regulation)
Bicycle bridge project over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue
Grant application for bicycle and pedestrian planning studies

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026 to get community input on a new rule or policy. After listening to residents, the council moved forward with the proposal by skipping the standard first review and planning to vote on it at their next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather resident feedback
New ordinance (city rule) being fast-tracked for adoption
Council skipped initial reading process to speed up approval
Final vote scheduled for next regular council meeting

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 7, 2026, to approve official records from several March meetings and authorize the Town Manager to sign a construction contract with QLM, Inc. The meeting focused on clearing administrative items and moving forward with a construction project, though the specific details of the construction work aren't fully described in the available information.
keyTopics: Approval of meeting minutes from multiple March sessions
Authorization of construction contract with QLM, Inc.
Town Manager granted signing authority for the construction project
Closed session and open session meeting records reviewed

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 21, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council met to approve several agreements and projects affecting the airport and city infrastructure. Key decisions included renewing a contract with Vanderlande Industries for airport baggage handling equipment, updating storm sewer system planning with Wood Rodgers Inc., and addressing affordability protections for housing when subsidy contracts end or properties face foreclosure.
keyTopics: Airport baggage conveyor system contract renewal with Vanderlande Industries
Storm sewer system master planning agreement update
Affordable housing protections if subsidy contracts expire or foreclosure occurs
FEMA San José Family Camp Project funding/actions

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 14, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a meeting to review the city's spending plans for the next fiscal year, including what construction and improvement projects they want to fund. The council also officially recognized Teen Month and National Volunteer Week to celebrate young people and volunteers in the community.
keyTopics: 2026-27 budget and capital improvement projects
Teen Month recognition and celebration
National Volunteer Week proclamation
City infrastructure and spending priorities

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 7, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held their regular meeting on April 7, 2026, where they recognized Arab-American Heritage Month and Fair Housing Month. The meeting opened with the traditional flag salute and included special acknowledgments of these important observances for the community.
keyTopics: Arab-American Heritage Month recognition
Fair Housing Month observance
City Council regular session
Community heritage celebrations

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 13, 2026
cupertino.summary: The City Council held a community workshop to help residents learn about and use OpenGov, a tool that shows how the city spends its money and makes its finances more transparent. The meeting also covered rules about who needs to register as a lobbyist if they try to influence city decisions.
keyTopics: OpenGov financial transparency tool demonstration and training
City budget access and how to track where your tax dollars go
Lobbyist registration requirements under municipal code Chapter 2.100
New reporting requirements for those influencing city decisions

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to approve routine administrative items and consider updates to the city's 5-year housing and community development plan. They also discussed a proposed change to rules about how council vacancies are handled.
keyTopics: Updates to the 2025-2030 housing and community development funding plan
New project requests from individual council members
Changes to the process for filling council vacancies
Approval of previous meeting minutes and upcoming meeting schedule

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 9, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council held a public hearing about a building at 405 Kipling Street in downtown. The owner is asking to officially recognize it as a historic building, which would allow them to add 5,000 extra square feet of floor space for renovations and earthquake safety improvements.
keyTopics: 405 Kipling Street historic designation upgrade (Category 4 to Category 2)
5,000 sq ft bonus floor area for seismic and historic rehabilitation
Downtown Commercial zone development
Historic Resources Board meeting schedule approval
March 12 board minutes review
